title: 对齐安装升级文档与真实行为

sources.reason: Remote installer currently defaults clone source independently from the public repository used by Quick Install.
Upgrade preflight compares schema integers but does not compare release semver.
Installer digest must be regenerated when the managed installer changes.
Release metadata must point to the regenerated provenance evidence digest.
English Quick Install contract and explicit public repository variables.
Chinese Quick Install uses placeholder defaults and omits installer repository propagation.
Japanese Quick Install uses placeholder defaults and omits installer repository propagation.
Installation docs describe status output and Quick Install behavior.
Upgrade docs promise semver downgrade protection.
Defines documentation and executable end-to-end acceptance requirements.

riskAssessment.reason: Users may install from the wrong repository or accept a lower release if documentation and implementation disagree; fixes must preserve backward-compatible local source behavior.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: A custom public repository configured in Quick Install is propagated to the tagged installer source selection.
Upgrade rejects a lower release semver before writing while preserving existing distribution and schema downgrade guards.
English, Chinese, and Japanese Quick Install examples expose equivalent executable variables and status semantics.

executionDecision.reason: The reported mismatches are reproducible from local docs and installer behavior and can be closed with implementation and executable tests.

preReviewWarnings: Verify all three language quick-install examples pass the public repository through to the installer and that lower release semver is rejected before writes.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: Quick Install passes the configured public repository through to the installer and supports custom mirrors.
No-active status documentation matches the actual check-ai-status output.
Upgrade rejects lower release semver before modifying the target.
English, Chinese, and Japanese Quick Install examples are executable and semantically consistent.
guidelines: New public APIs must be documented.
Do not import internal modules from other features.

intent.problem: Documentation claims custom public repository routing, no-active status output, semver downgrade protection, and multilingual executable parity that the current installer and examples do not consistently implement.
constraints: Preserve explicit local source and repository overrides for existing installations.
Reject unsafe upgrades before any target files are written.
Keep documentation examples free of machine-specific or private repository values.
intent.rationale: Treating documentation examples as executable contracts exposes routing and upgrade drift before users encounter it and makes the three language entry points operationally equivalent.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: User explicitly requested the remediation loop to continue until all listed review blockers are completed.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
